justification: All pass criteria met: both queues (order.fulfillment, order.fulfillment.dlq) identified by name; both env vars (RABBITMQ_URL, FULFILLMENT_DLQ_NAME) identified by name; escalations routed to Coach K with specific infrastructure items listed; new_infrastructure_required section fully populated with new_queues_topics and new_env_vars; verdict is NOT READY; observability review correctly notes logging and p99 latency are adequate while identifying gaps in queue depth and DLQ metrics as separate concerns rather than false-positiving on the baseline.

observations.text: Both queues and both env vars identified by exact name in escalation messages and in new_infrastructure_required schema
Verdict is NOT READY with infrastructure gap as the hard blocker
Escalations correctly routed to Coach K, not to Shaq or Bird or MJ for the infrastructure items
new_infrastructure_required section is richly populated beyond minimum requirements — includes config_changes and infra_provisioning details
Observability review correctly identifies logging and p99 latency as adequate baseline, separating infrastructure blocker from observability gaps
Goes beyond rubric requirements with detailed resilience assessment, rollback risk analysis, and multiple failure mode scenarios

Some escalations route sub-items to Shaq and MJ (observability gap to Shaq, integration risk to MJ) — these are secondary escalations through Coach K which is acceptable, but the routing notation 'Coach K → Shaq' could be misread as routing to Shaq directly
